name: Lokshen Kugeel -- Noodle Pudding
recipeIngredient: 3 cups sour cream (even a little less will do)
4 to 6 eggs, depending on how rich you like it (go for broke)
1 lb broad or thin noodles, cooked
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, combine cottage cheese, sour cream, milk, and half of the melted margarine.
2. Beat together eggs, sugar, and vanilla in a separate bowl.
3. Add the egg mixture to the cottage cheese mixture.
4. Stir in the cooked noodles and raisins.
5. Pour into a large buttered pan or two smaller ones.
6. Top with remaining melted margarine.
7. Mix together cinnamon and sugar; sprinkle on top.
8. Optionally, use crushed cornflakes mixed with cinnamon and sugar as topping.
9. Bake at 350°F for about an hour, or until lightly browned.
10. This dish can be frozen and reheated later.
Note: For convenience, you may mix all ingredients together in a single large bowl using a wire whisk. The recipe yields a generous quantity suitable for a main dish or side serving. It remains delicious at room temperature, so heating is not necessary during buffet service.
